title: Aluminum Prices Ease After Iran Shock as Trade Pressure Keeps Costs in Focus
summary: Aluminum futures traded around $3,400 per ton, falling below peak-crisis forecasts that circulated earlier in the Iran-US conflict. The price decline reflects easing tensions following the shock, though trade pressure continues to keep costs in focus for the market.

title: Aluminum price falls on US-Iran deal but supply normalization to take time: analysts
summary: Analysts report that aluminum prices dropped following the US-Iran deal, yet they caution that normalizing global supply will take time. The market is also seeing aluminum recycling accelerate as a shift from a green initiative to a necessity.

title: Aluminum Prices Are Giving Up Some Gains Despite Gulf Disruptions
summary: In early U.S. trading on Thursday, aluminum prices retreated 6% to $3,214.50, despite ongoing disruptions in the Middle East jeopardizing supply chains. A reduction in demand coupled with increasing inventories in China is moderating some of the previous price increases.

title: AI data centers' massive demand for aluminum is crushing the US aluminum industry
summary: The article highlights a new aluminum smelter planned in Oklahoma with a capacity of 600,000 tons per year, which would nearly double current US domestic production. It notes that only six smelting operations remain in the US, with just four actively producing approximately 670,000 tons in 2024, accounting for less than 1% of global output.

title: Major US aluminum producer boosts production to full capacity
summary: Fox News reports on Century Aluminum's decision to boost production to full capacity amid President Donald Trump's tariffs on aluminum. The move is part of a broader strategy to increase domestic aluminum production and reduce reliance on foreign suppliers.

title: US aluminium smelters vie with Big Tech for scarce power
summary: The article discusses the intense competition between US aluminium smelters and Big Tech companies for scarce electricity, noting that producing a ton of aluminium requires 14,821 kilowatt-hours. It reveals that only six smelters remain operational, with two completely shut down and others operating below potential, resulting in annual production plummeting to 700,000 tons.

title: Aluminum hits four-year high on Mideast supply loss
summary: This video report explains that aluminum prices reached a four-year high due to supply deficits caused by attacks in the Middle East, with an estimated 3 million tons shortfall in 2026. The segment projects substantial deficits for the current year and next, as declines in the Middle East cannot be offset by other sources.

title: US primary aluminum growth a 'function of energy'
summary: Fastmarkets reports that US primary aluminum growth depends heavily on energy costs, with four smelters producing 683,500 tonnes of aluminum. The article emphasizes that rising energy costs and policy decisions are shaping the future of US aluminium smelting, with power supply challenges influencing investment decisions.

title: Aluminum prices rise after Iran attacks Gulf smelters
summary: CNBC reports that aluminum prices surged to a four-year high following Iranian attacks on two Gulf smelters, raising fears of a global supply shortage. Commodities strategist Joyce Li noted that prior to the attacks, the base scenario anticipated a 20% reduction in running capacity, equating to a production loss of 800 to 900 kilotons in 2026.

title: America's big new aluminum smelter is still waiting on a power deal
summary: Canary Media reports that America's new aluminum smelter is still awaiting a finalized power deal, with construction expected to begin in late 2026 and metal production by the end of the decade. The article underscores the critical role of securing affordable and reliable power for the project's success.

title: 3 technologies helping the aluminum industry decarbonize
summary: This article highlights Hydro's HalZero technology, which eliminates CO2 emissions during aluminum smelting by keeping carbon and chlorine in a closed loop. It also covers innovations in recycling metal impurities from aluminum and using hydrogen as an alternative to natural gas in alumina refining.

title: New filter captures and recycles aluminum from manufacturing waste
summary: MIT engineers developed a nanofiltration process that captures over 99% of aluminum ions from hazardous waste streams in aluminum production. The captured aluminum can be upcycled into bulk production, increasing yield while reducing waste.

title: New & Innovative Markets
summary: The U.S. Aluminum Industry Sector Snapshot report shows positive environmental impact trendlines, indicating the industry is becoming more sustainable than ever through innovative market applications.

title: Aluminum: Driving Efficiency and Sustainability Across All Vehicle Segments
summary: Recycling aluminum uses approximately 95% less energy than primary production, resulting in 95% fewer carbon emissions. This efficiency makes aluminum a key driver of sustainability in vehicle manufacturing across all segments.

title: Zero-carbon aluminium is the future of aluminium production
summary: Hydro has cut emissions by 55% since 1990, positioning zero-carbon aluminum as essential for the green transition. The video emphasizes that aluminum production must be carbon emission-free to enable sustainable development.

title: Moving Aluminum Production Into the Future
summary: Technology upgrades for producing sustainable aluminum are already in use, addressing the industry's need for an upgrade. These innovations promise to transform aluminum production into a more sustainable process.

title: Innovations in Aluminum Manufacturing: What's New in 2024?
summary: Cutting-edge technologies like AI, IoT, and advanced robotics are reshaping the aluminum production landscape. These innovations are driving efficiency and sustainability in the industry.

title: Aluminum industries latest news
summary: Global aluminum demand is surging due to electric vehicles, renewable energy, construction, and sustainable packaging. This trend reflects the growing importance of aluminum in sustainable industries.

title: US aluminum, steel prices jump on Trump's new tariffs
date: Monday (tariffs set to take effect June 4th)
summary: US steel and aluminum prices soared after President Donald Trump announced he would double tariffs on the two metals to 50%, intensifying the global trade war. Foreign steelmaker shares slumped as the new tariffs are set to take effect on June 4th.

title: American Steel Just Got More Expensive. Buyers Blame Tariffs.
summary: Cleveland-Cliffs increased its average steel price to $1,015 per ton in the second quarter, up from $980 in the first, as manufacturers pass tariff costs to domestic producers. The latest tariffs on steel and aluminum by President Trump have caused a recent surge in US steel prices despite market stabilization.

title: Steel rose to 3064 CNY/T on June 26, 2026
summary: Steel prices rose to 3064 CNY/T on June 26, 2026, marking a 0.20% increase from the previous day. Over the past month, steel prices have fallen 2.95%, though they remain 3.44% higher than the previous year.

title: Latest Steel Prices (June 2026)
summary: Current steel prices for June 2026 show Hot-Rolled Coil at $1,109/ton, Cold Rolled Steel at $1,293/ton, and Coated Steel at $1,380/ton. Steel Plate is priced at $1,217/ton, reflecting recent market trends in the steel industry.

title: Steel Production Dips in the U.S. | #ScrapMonsterNews
summary: The latest data from AISI shows a 2.4% dip in weekly U.S. raw steel production. The Southern region continues to face challenges as utilization rates soften.

title: Steel imports down 30% in 2026 as tariffs bolster US production
summary: Foreign steel entering the United States was nearly 6% higher in April but remains about 30% lower year-to-date due to Section 232 tariffs disrupting trade flows. These tariffs continue to support domestic steel production.

title: Global steel industry battles surplus supply, protectionism
summary: Steelmaking capacity is expected to rise for a seventh consecutive year, reaching 2.55 billion metric tons by the end of 2025, according to the OECD steel report. Planned additions in Asia and the Middle East will bring an additional 109 million mt of new capacity by 2028, reinforcing structural overcapacity.

title: Current State of the U.S. Steel Market
summary: Capacity utilization has slipped to 74.6%, falling short of the administration's stated goal of 80.0%, while domestic production has seen a slowdown. Recent short-term indicators suggest stabilization, with production levels 4.1% higher than the year-to-date average for the week of May 10, 2025.

title: Latest developments in steelmaking capacity and outlook until 2026
summary: World crude steelmaking capacity in 2023 is estimated at 2,432 million tonnes, exceeding global production by 543 million tonnes. Between 2024 and 2026, the global steel industry will see an additional 68.3 million mt of capacity coming on stream, with gross additions potentially reaching 157.0 million mt.

title: Industry Data - American Iron and Steel Institute
summary: In the week ending June 20, 2026, domestic raw steel production was 1,851,000 net tons while the capability utilization rate was 80.2 percent. This marks a significant increase in utilization compared to previous periods.

title: 3 innovations in green steel
summary: Boston Metal, a Bill Gates-backed startup, raised $50 million to develop a CO2-free steel process using molten oxide electrolysis. Sweden is investing $3.99 billion to build a commercial hydrogen steel plant, while Kobe Steel introduced a new technique for steel production in natural gas blast furnaces that reduces coke usage.

title: Smelting Barriers: The Innovators Forging a New Future for Iron and Steel
summary: Northern Sweden will produce the first commercial volumes of near-zero emissions steel, marking a breakthrough in direct electrification and material feedstock innovations. Companies like GreenOre are monetizing steel slag waste streams to create supplementary cementitious materials, reducing environmental pollution and accelerating commercial traction.

title: Green transformation in the iron and steel industry in India
summary: New technologies are being developed in India that significantly cut carbon emissions in the iron and steel sector, with steel majors actively pursuing various technology avenues and seeking relevant partners. These innovations aim to make the industry more sustainable and economically viable while addressing climate goals.

title: Innovations in Heat & Water Recovery for a Greener Industry
summary: The Heat Pipe Condensing Economiser (HPCE) technology is transforming the steel sector by improving heat and water recovery, making it more sustainable and economically viable. This innovation helps reduce energy consumption and emissions in industrial steel production processes.

title: The Future of Steel: Innovation and Sustainability with U. S. Steel
summary: United States Steel Corporation unveils the pivotal role of steel in modern manufacturing and its contribution to sustainability through innovation. The video highlights how steel enables lighter, more fuel-efficient vehicles and taller, safer buildings while advancing green technology.

title: Copper prices are surging, driven by a confluence of factors including geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand
summary: Copper prices are surging due to geopolitical tensions, structural supply shortages, and booming AI-driven demand. The metal is increasingly viewed as a strategic commodity for the AI era, with markets anticipating a prolonged supply crunch due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects.

title: Copper prices reach a new record high after US announces copper tariffs aligned with existing 50% on steel, aluminium
summary: Copper prices hit a record high after the US announced 50% tariffs on copper, aligned with existing tariffs on steel and aluminum. At one point, prices surged 17% to hit an all-time high of around $5.9 a barrel, with US copper trading at a record premium of $2,750 a ton.

title: Copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online fast enough to meet rising demand
summary: Copper's long-term outlook is constrained by the industry's limited ability to bring new supply online quickly to meet rising demand, with permitting delays further complicating the situation. This supply crunch is expected to persist due to underinvestment and long lead times for new mining projects.

title: Nickel Prices Ready to Rise as Supply Tightens in Fourth Quarter
summary: The global nickel market is reaching a critical inflection point as supply and demand dynamics converge, potentially ending the prolonged range-bound pricing between $15,000-$15,800 per ton that persisted since April 2025[1]. Indonesia's strategic shift represents the most significant development in nickel markets, with supply tightening expected in the fourth quarter[1].

title: Nickel Futures Price Today - Investing.com
summary: Nickel prices rose on Tuesday, with the current price of Nickel futures at 16,795.63 and a trading range between 16,573.25 and 16,861.00[2]. The spot price of Nickel today is 16,716.88, while the current futures price is 16,731.38 with a previous close of 16,860.88[2].

title: Nickel Prices 2026 - Chart, Trends Analysis & Forecast - IMARC Group
summary: As of May 2026, Northeast Asia nickel prices reached USD 21.45/kg (up 13.3%) and Europe prices reached USD 19.58/kg (up 19.5%)[3]. In April 2026, prices were USD 18.93/kg in Northeast Asia, USD 16.39/kg in Europe, and USD 16.49/kg in North America, all showing downward movement[3].

title: Nickel Market Faces New Reality as Indonesia's Ore Quality Slips
summary: The global nickel market is entering a more complex phase driven by structural changes in Indonesia, where average nickel ore content fell from 1.66% in 2024 to 1.52% in 2025[4]. Authorities are informally targeting a nickel price range of $18,000 to $21,000 per tonne in the near term, with a pickup in mergers and acquisitions signaling renewed strategic interest in nickel assets[4].

title: LME Nickel | London Metal Exchange
summary: The current LME Nickel price is 16,699.00, representing a 0.74% decrease from the previous session[5]. The London Metal Exchange provides current and historical nickel prices, stocks, and monthly averages for market analysis[5].

title: Nickel Spot Price Chart | Live Price of Nickel per Ounce
summary: The nickel spot price is currently 16,660.00, with a previous close of 14,116.50[6]. The 52-week low is 19,602.50 and the 52-week high is 16,570.00, indicating recent volatility in nickel pricing[6].

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News - Trading Economics
summary: Nickel fell to $16,700 per tonne on June 26, 2026, nearing its lowest level since late December[7]. The price dropped to 16,805 USD/T on June 26, 2026, down 0.36% from the previous day[7].

title: Price of Nickel per Pound - KITCO
summary: KITCO provides live nickel charts and spot prices from international markets including New York, London, Hong Kong, and Sydney[8]. The platform offers real-time data on nickel prices per pound for traders and analysts[8].

title: Nickel prices, news and market analysis - Fastmarkets
summary: Fastmarkets offers the latest nickel prices, trends, charts, news, and market analysis from expert price reporters[9]. The platform provides comprehensive nickel market data for industry professionals and investors[9].

title: Nickel Price - Nickel Rate in India Today Live on The Economic Times
summary: The Economic Times reports live nickel prices in India, with the London Metal Exchange electronic trading resuming after a nearly 3-hour outage[10]. The current nickel price in India is ₹184.40 per unit[10].

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: Indonesia has notified PT Weda Bay Nickel, the world's largest nickel mine, to drastically cut its ore production quota from 42 million tons in 2025 to 12 million tons in 2026 to boost global prices. The company plans to apply for a revision of this decision, which has already caused nickel prices to climb as the market reacts to potential supply shortages.

title: Indonesia navigates nickel market with output cuts, policy shifts
summary: Indonesia's Minister of Energy and Mineral Resources confirmed plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to support prices and government revenue while cracking down on environmentally harmful operations. The country has reversed its mining quota policy to regulate supply volatility, with actual production currently lagging behind approved quotas.

title: Can nickel prices hit $25,000 in 2026?
summary: The Oregon Group projects nickel prices could reach $25,000 per tonne if supply tightens, noting that Indonesia's approved ore capacity was only 55% utilized in 2025, revealing a 'phantom surplus.' Indonesia has signaled major supply cuts for 2026, including banning new smelters and shortening quota validity to one year to manage pricing.

title: Boom-Bust-Bounce: Understanding Nickel's Latest Price Cycle
summary: Global nickel prices settled at $16,775.98 per ton on June 26, 2026, showing a fractional 0.11% gain despite a recent 5.11% drop. While the Oregon Group predicts a potential rise to $25,000 if supply tightens, analysts currently view a stable range between $20,000 and $22,000 per tonne as more realistic.

title: Unleashing sustainable nickel production for a net zero future
summary: This article investigates how optimizing nickel production can create a globally sustainable battery industry by implementing technologies that reduce greenhouse gas emissions and improve energy efficiency. It emphasizes that enhancing stakeholder engagement and robust ESG disclosure standards are essential for promoting transparency in the global nickel sector.

title: Green nickel for sustainable electrification
summary: Researchers at the Max Planck Institute for Sustainable Materials developed a carbon-free, energy-saving method for nickel extraction using hydrogen plasma instead of carbon, which cuts CO2 emissions by 84%. This single-step process enables the efficient processing of low-grade nickel ores, which account for 60% of total reserves, directly producing refined ferronickel alloy.

title: Clean energy's nickel rush is heading straight for some of Earth's richest ecosystems
summary: Meeting future nickel demand for stainless steel and clean energy technologies will require tough decisions with potential environmental impacts on Earth's richest ecosystems. The article highlights the urgent need for sustainable practices as the nickel industry expands to support the global transition to net-zero energy.

title: Italy's Prysmian sees stronger U.S. pricing, margins after new copper tariff scheme
summary: Prysmian, an Italian cable manufacturer, is poised to gain from the newly established copper tariff framework in the United States, which imposes duties on imported cables. The company's CEO stated that the new levies on foreign-made cables will benefit the group's pricing and margins in the U.S. market.

title: Prysmian shares rise as Italian cable maker seen as winner amid US copper tariffs
summary: Prysmian shares rose as much as 4% on Wednesday due to expectations that the world's largest cable maker's large U.S. business could benefit from U.S. tariffs on copper. The market reaction reflects investor confidence in the company's ability to capitalize on the new tariff environment.

title: Prysmian receives the notice to proceed for the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector
summary: Prysmian has officially received the notice to proceed for the construction of the Italy-Tunisia electrical interconnector, a major infrastructure project. This development underscores Prysmian's continued role in advancing energy connectivity between Europe and North Africa.
